tools.name: analyze_convergence
tools.description: Answer 'do I have enough scans?' via cosine similarity of each scan to the running mean, plus cumulative convergence and standard error.
tools.when_to_use: Check if repeated scans have converged using cosine similarity metrics. Reports per-scan similarity to the mean, cumulative convergence, and standard error. Cosine similarity is amplitude-dominated; the post-edge plateau (defined to be ~1.0 by edge-step normalization) dominates the metric. You must pass e_min/e_max to focus on the dynamic part of the spectrum.

tools.name: analyze_efficiency
tools.description: Comprehensive scan-repetition efficiency report: convergence, coefficient of variation, comparison to the Poisson statistical limit, and a terminal verdict (needs_more / reasonable / marginal / wasteful).
tools.when_to_use: Comprehensive scan repetition efficiency report. Includes convergence, CV analysis, rate-based and counts-based Poisson floor comparison, optimal scan count recommendation, and a verdict (needs_more / reasonable / marginal / wasteful). e_min/e_max bounds are required: the verdict is only meaningful on the dynamic feature window (white-line / pre-edge), since the normalization-defined plateaus otherwise dominate the statistics and mask a feature that is still resolving.

tools.name: analyze_feature_evolution
tools.description: Per-rep scalar trace + convergence verdict for a feature defined by an energy window and a statistic. The agent identifies a feature on the spectrum (white-line peak, pre-edge shoulder, dip between oscillations, etc.) and passes the numeric eV bounds and the statistic that captures it. Returns running mean, running SEM, and a verdict (converged / marginal / needs_more) for that scalar. This is the publication-quality test: the feature SEM should be a small fraction of its mean and the running mean should be flat rep-over-rep.
tools.when_to_use: Per-rep scalar trace + convergence verdict for a feature defined by an energy window and a statistic. The agent identifies a feature on the spectrum (white-line peak, pre-edge shoulder, dip between oscillations, etc.) and passes the numeric eV bounds and the statistic that captures it. Returns running mean, running SEM, and a verdict (converged / marginal / needs_more) for that scalar. This is the publication-quality test: the feature SEM should be a small fraction of its mean and the running mean should be flat rep-over-rep.

tools.name: analyze_per_spot
tools.description: Run the full convergence/efficiency analysis SEPARATELY for each sample spot in the file (grouped by Sx/Sy/Sz), and report a between-spot vs within-spot heterogeneity F-statistic. F~1 = spots agree (safe to combine); F>>1 = spots disagree beyond shot noise (the combined average is a population mean, not a single chemistry — more reps won't fix it). Pass numeric e_min/e_max for the feature you care about.
tools.when_to_use: Run the full convergence/efficiency analysis SEPARATELY for each sample spot in the file (grouped by Sx/Sy/Sz), and report a between-spot vs within-spot heterogeneity F-statistic. F~1 = spots agree (safe to combine); F>>1 = spots disagree beyond shot noise (the combined average is a population mean, not a single chemistry — more reps won't fix it). Pass numeric e_min/e_max for the feature you care about.

tools.name: average_scans
tools.description: Edge-step normalize every energy scan in a file, then average them. Returns the mean, the point-wise standard deviation, and the number of scans averaged.
tools.when_to_use: Average all energy scans in a SPEC file after edge-step normalization. Returns mean and standard deviation across scans. If file_name is omitted, uses the most recent file with >1 energy scan. Optionally crops the average to a numeric energy window [e_min, e_max] in eV, and supports SNR-aware inverse-variance weighting across reps.

tools.name: capture_sample_image
tools.description: Capture a low-resolution JPEG snapshot from the RPi-Cam sample camera. Returns image metadata as text and the raw JPEG as an inline base64 image. Also logs the JPEG to DATA_DIR/camera_log/ for audit. Useful for visual inspection of sample position, beam spot location, or cryostat window state.
tools.when_to_use: Capture a low-resolution JPEG snapshot of the sample from the beamline sample camera (RPi-Cam). Returns image metadata as text and the JPEG as an inline image. Use this to visually inspect sample position, beam spot, or cryostat window before or during alignment and data collection.

tools.name: evaluate_spec_macro
tools.description: Run a SPEC macro in a disposable, network-isolated Docker container and return the execution log. Use to validate macros you authored or edited, or to reproduce a SPEC error in isolation. Each call is a cold start with no state from prior runs. The container has read-only access to production beamline macros but no network and no host devices — it cannot affect the live beamline. Always read the log even on ok=True; SPEC sometimes exits 0 despite warnings.
tools.when_to_use: Run a SPEC macro in a disposable, network-isolated sandbox container. Returns JSON with an `output` key containing the clean command result and a `log` key with the full session transcript (startup noise included). Use `output` for parsing; use `log` only for debugging. Each call is a cold start: no state persists between calls. Sim-only — does not affect real hardware. Always check `output` even on ok=True; SPEC sometimes exits 0 despite warnings.
tools.sample_output: {"ok": true, "exit_code": 0, "timed_out": false, "output": "hello from sandbox\n", "log": "(full session transcript)", "duration_s": 0.42, "run_id": "abc123def456", "error": null}

tools.name: execute_readonly_sql
tools.description: Run a read-only SELECT against the S3DF metadata Postgres. Rejects write keywords; truncates at max_rows.
tools.when_to_use: Run a read-only SELECT against the S3DF metadata Postgres. Rejects write keywords; truncates at max_rows.

tools.name: get_active_counter
tools.description: Pick the 'meaningful' counter for an energy scan. Heuristic: ppboff if it exists, else the vortDT/vortDT2/vortDT3/vortDT4 with the highest max count, else I1.
tools.when_to_use: Identify the active fluorescence/absorption counter for a scan stored in S3DF.

tools.name: get_counter_config
tools.description: Return SPEC's counter table: per-counter controller, unit, channel, scale, flags, and mnemonic. The counter index (CNTnnn) maps to the S[] array.
tools.when_to_use: Get SPEC counter configuration from the config file. Shows controller, unit, channel, scale, flags, mnemonic, and name for each counter. Counter index (CNTnnn) maps to the S[] array.
tools.sample_output:
CNT000  EPICS_SC  0  0  1  0x001  sec  Seconds
CNT001  EPICS_SC  0  1  1  0x002  I0   I0
CNT002  EPICS_SC  0  2  1  0x000  I1   I1
...

tools.name: get_holder_time_budget
tools.description: Return the time budget for one or all holders: beamtime_hours, stop_time (absolute deadline), and hours_remaining (computed). Read-only.
tools.when_to_use: Return the time budget for one or all holders: beamtime_hours, stop_time (absolute deadline), and hours_remaining (computed). Read-only.

tools.name: get_latest_log_entries
tools.description: Return the tail of the beamline control log. The agent uses this to see what SPEC just printed — prompts, warnings, the text of recent commands.
tools.when_to_use: Get the most recent entries from the beamline control logs.

tools.name: get_latest_scan
tools.description: Return the most recently modified SPEC scan on disk, along with its metadata (file, scan number, command, counters) and a small numeric preview of the scan's points.
tools.when_to_use: Return metadata for the most-recently inserted scan in the S3DF Postgres metadata table.

tools.name: get_motor_config
tools.description: Return SPEC's motor table: per-motor controller, steps/unit, slew rate, flags, and mnemonic. The motor index (MOTnnn) maps directly to the A[] array in SPEC.
tools.when_to_use: Get SPEC motor configuration from the config file. Shows controller, steps/unit, slew rate, flags, mnemonic, and name for each motor. Motor index (MOTnnn) maps to the A[] array.
tools.sample_output:
MOT000  EPICS_M1  2000  1.0  0x003  th     Theta
MOT001  EPICS_M1  2000  1.0  0x003  phi    Phi
MOT002  EPICS_M1  2000  1.0  0x003  chi    Chi
...

tools.name: get_reference_image
tools.description: Return a reference image for a known sample environment or diagnostic tool. The image is read from version-controlled package data and returned inline as base64 JPEG. Compare the result with a live capture_sample_image snapshot to confirm what is currently mounted on the sample stage.
tools.when_to_use: Return a reference image for a known sample environment or diagnostic tool. Compare the result with a live capture_sample_image snapshot to confirm what is currently mounted on the sample stage.

tools.name: get_scan_deadtime
tools.description: Compute how much of a scan was acquisition vs overhead (motor moves, settling, comms). Useful when optimizing count time or diagnosing slow scans.
tools.when_to_use: Get dead-time stats for a scan (wall-clock vs acquisition seconds and percentage).

tools.name: group_scans_by_spot
tools.description: Cluster a file's scans by sample spot using the recorded Sx/Sy/Sz motor positions. Two scans are the same spot if their Sx, Sy, Sz all agree within tol_mm. Useful before convergence analysis when reps came from multiple spots — between-spot differences can pollute the combined cross-spot CV. Pair with analyze_per_spot.
tools.when_to_use: Cluster a file's scans by sample spot using the recorded Sx/Sy/Sz motor positions. Two scans are the same spot if their Sx, Sy, Sz all agree within tol_mm. Useful before convergence analysis when reps came from multiple spots — between-spot differences can pollute the combined cross-spot CV. Pair with analyze_per_spot.

tools.name: list_channels
tools.description: List public Slack channels the bot belongs to.
tools.when_to_use: List public Slack channels the bot belongs to.

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List non-SPEC files (macros, configs, notes) in the scan directory so the agent can decide what to read or edit.
tools.when_to_use: List non-SPEC files in the scan directory (macros, configs, text files).

tools.name: list_logs
tools.description: List the available log files in BL_LOGS_DIR with their sizes and modification times.
tools.when_to_use: List available log files.

tools.name: list_scans
tools.description: Enumerate recent SPEC scans with metadata so the agent can pick a file_name/scan_number pair for follow-up tools.
tools.when_to_use: List processed scans from the S3DF Postgres metadata table, most-recent first.

tools.name: log_status_assessment
tools.description: Append the planner's STATUS ASSESSMENT block to a per-experiment JSONL file under logs/. Canonical record of the per-spawn assessment — separate from Slack.
tools.when_to_use: Append the planner's STATUS ASSESSMENT block to logs/status_assessments_<experiment_id>.jsonl. File-only record (does not post to Slack).

tools.name: normalize_scan
tools.description: Edge-step normalize an energy scan: divide the signal by I0 (or any chosen reference), then linearly rescale so the pre-edge reads 0 and the post-edge reads 1.
tools.when_to_use: Edge-step normalize a scan: divide signal by I0, then scale so pre-edge is 0 and post-edge is 1. Returns the normalized data array.

tools.name: plot_averaged_scans
tools.description: Edge-step normalize every scan in each given SPEC file, average, and overlay all samples on one plot with std-dev shading. The go-to cross-sample comparison plot.
tools.when_to_use: Plot averaged energy scans for multiple samples overlaid on one plot. Each sample is edge-step normalized and averaged, then plotted with standard deviation shading.
tools.sample_output: Overlay plot of 2 samples (Fe_HERFD_0001: 5 scans averaged, Fe_HERFD_0002: 3 scans averaged). [PNG image returned]

tools.name: plot_data
tools.description: General-purpose line plotter. The agent passes raw arrays — typically grabbed from read_scan or normalize_scan — and gets back a rendered PNG. Supports up to four overlaid series.
tools.when_to_use: General-purpose plotting tool. Plot any data as a line chart. Use this to visualize results from other tools (e.g. read_scan). Supports multiple series on one plot.
tools.sample_output: Plot generated: Fe K-edge XANES (5 points, 1 series). [PNG image returned]

tools.name: plot_feature_evolution
tools.description: Plot a single per-rep scalar (the chosen statistic over [e_min, e_max]) versus rep number, with running mean and ±SEM band. The visual companion to analyze_feature_evolution. Use to confirm a feature has flatlined; a still-trending trace means the feature is not yet converged.
tools.when_to_use: Plot a single per-rep scalar (the chosen statistic over [e_min, e_max]) versus rep number, with running mean and ±SEM band. The visual companion to analyze_feature_evolution. Use to confirm a feature has flatlined; a still-trending trace means the feature is not yet converged.

tools.name: plot_first_half_vs_second_half
tools.description: Compare the average of the first half of reps to the second half, with SEM bands. Reports max |Δ|/SEM. <2σ: halves agree, sample is stationary. >3σ at any feature: the halves disagree, more reps may not help (drift, damage, or heterogeneity). This is the strongest single-glance test for whether the sample is publication-clean.
tools.when_to_use: Compare the average of the first half of reps to the second half, with SEM bands. Reports max |Δ|/SEM. <2σ: halves agree, sample is stationary. >3σ at any feature: the halves disagree, more reps may not help (drift, damage, or heterogeneity). This is the strongest single-glance test for whether the sample is publication-clean.

tools.name: plot_running_average
tools.description: Plot the running average across reps as it evolves (one line per cumulative subset, color-progressed by rep #), with the final ±SEM band. Shows whether the running mean is still changing rep-over-rep at the feature of interest.
tools.when_to_use: Plot the running average across reps as it evolves (one line per cumulative subset, color-progressed by rep #), with the final ±SEM band. Shows whether the running mean is still changing rep-over-rep at the feature of interest.

tools.name: plot_scan
tools.description: Render one scan as a PNG and return it to the user. Auto-detects the active counter; accepts an optional normalize_by counter.
tools.when_to_use: Plot one scan from the S3DF pickle store. Auto-detects the active counter when none is given. The plot is shown directly to the user.
tools.sample_output: Fe_HERFD_0001 scan #3: ascan energy 7090 7200 301 1.0 (counter: vortDT). [PNG image returned]

tools.name: plot_scan_stack
tools.description: Overlay all reps of one sample on a single axis, color-progressed by rep order. Use to visually judge whether reps scatter symmetrically around a stable mean (converged), are still drifting in one direction (more reps needed or evolving sample), or are being burned away (damage). Pass numeric e_min/e_max to crop to the feature you care about.
tools.when_to_use: Overlay all reps of one sample on a single axis, color-progressed by rep order. Use to visually judge whether reps scatter symmetrically around a stable mean (converged), are still drifting in one direction (more reps needed or evolving sample), or are being burned away (damage). Pass numeric e_min/e_max to crop to the feature you care about.

tools.name: post_slack_message
tools.description: Post a message to a Slack channel or thread reply.
tools.when_to_use: Post a message to a Slack channel or thread reply.

tools.name: post_status_update
tools.description: Post a high-level progress message to Slack and the dashboard feed. Informational — does not block or gate anything.
tools.when_to_use: Post a high-level progress message to Slack + UI.

tools.name: read_channel_messages
tools.description: Read recent messages from a Slack channel.
tools.when_to_use: Read recent messages from a Slack channel.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a text file from the scan directory — typically a .mac macro the agent wants to inspect or edit.
tools.when_to_use: Read a text file from the scan directory. Use list_files to discover available files.
tools.sample_output:
# run01.mac - Fe HERFD collection
def run01 '
  newfile Fe_HERFD_0001
  Fe_xas 1.0 5
'

tools.name: read_scan
tools.description: Return the full data array of a single scan, addressed by file_name + scan_number. Use list_scans first to discover the valid identifiers.
tools.when_to_use: Read a processed scan's metadata + DataFrame from the converter's pickle store. Use s3df list-scans to discover file_name and scan_number.
tools.sample_output:
{"file_name": "Fe_HERFD_0001", "scan_number": 3, "command": "ascan energy 7090 7200 301 1.0", "counters": ["energy", "Seconds", "I0", "I1", "vortDT"], "data": "   energy  Seconds       I0      I1   vortDT
0  7090.0      1.0  125340    3201    412
1  7091.0      1.0  125122    3198    418
..."}

tools.name: read_thread_replies
tools.description: Read all replies in a Slack thread (parent + children).
tools.when_to_use: Read all replies in a Slack thread (parent + children).

tools.name: save_plan
tools.description: Persist a markdown plan into the project's logs/plans/ directory. Used at the start of multi-step tasks (typically beamline optimization) to record the step-by-step plan the agent intends to follow, so future sessions can review what was attempted and why. Filenames are validated against a strict allow-list and writes are confined to PLANS_DIR — no path traversal, no overwriting unless explicitly requested.
tools.when_to_use: Save a markdown plan to the project's logs/plans/ directory. Use this at the start of a beamline-optimization session (or any multi-step task) to persist the step-by-step plan you generated, so future sessions can review what was attempted and why.

tools.name: search_logs
tools.description: Grep-like search across beamline control logs for a literal string (error message, motor name, macro name). Returns a bounded match list.
tools.when_to_use: Search the beamline control logs for a specific string or error message.
tools.sample_output: [{"log_file": "spec_log_20260504.log", "line_number": 1423, "line": "ERROR: motor limit hit on th at 45.002"}, {"log_file": "spec_log_20260504.log", "line_number": 1425, "line": "WARNING: motor limit check failed for th"}]

tools.name: write_macro
tools.description: Save an edited SPEC macro under a new name with a _heroic_<date> suffix so the original macro is never overwritten.
tools.when_to_use: Save an edited macro as a new .mac file in the scan directory. The file is saved with a _heroic_<date> suffix to preserve the original.

tools.name: write_summary
tools.description: Persist a conversation summary into the scan directory as a timestamped .txt file. Used so operators can review agent reasoning offline.
tools.when_to_use: Save a conversation summary as a timestamped .txt file in the scan directory.

tools.name: get_comprehensive_collection_plan
tools.description: Return the per-sample/spot/filter/n_scans plan that Data Collection executes against. Synthesizes from ExperimentPlan.plan_json + SamplePosition rows + survey results (xas_filter, survey_counts_per_sec). planned_scans_total comes from plan_json's per-sample entry when set, falling back to xas_reps.
tools.when_to_use: Return the per-sample/spot/filter/n_scans plan that Data Collection executes against. Synthesizes from ExperimentPlan.plan_json plus SamplePosition rows (filter_count = xas_filter, counts_per_sec = survey_counts_per_sec). planned_scans_total comes from plan_json when set, falling back to xas_reps.

tools.name: get_experiment_config
tools.description: Return the operator-entered experiment configuration from the DB: experiment-level settings (mono crystal, beam, mirrors, sample env, data path), the configured elements, and every sample holder with its samples. This is the canonical record of what the user entered in the /config form — distinct from get_plan, which returns the live planner state.
tools.when_to_use: Return the operator-entered experiment configuration straight from the DB: experiment-level settings (mono crystal, beam size, mirrors, sample env, data path), the configured elements (edges, energies, crystal/HKL, vortex counter mnemonic — vortDT/vortDT2/vortDT3/vortDT4), and every sample holder with its samples (positions, gains, XAS/RIXS plan). Use this when you need ground truth from the /config form, independent of the live plan JSON.

tools.name: get_plan
tools.description: Return the live experiment plan — config, sample queue, holder budgets, and the beamtime budget.
tools.when_to_use: Return the live experiment plan (config + sample queue + budget).

tools.name: get_remaining_beamtime
tools.description: Hours from now until Experiment.end_time. Returns {remaining_hours, end_time}; both null if end_time has not been set yet.
tools.when_to_use: Hours from now until Experiment.end_time. Returns {remaining_hours, end_time} — or both null with a note if the operator has not yet called set_experiment_end_time.

tools.name: get_scans_for_active_sample
tools.description: Return every CollectionScan for the currently-active sample. Active = plan_json.active_sample_id (if set) else the lowest-queue-order sample whose status is not 'done' or 'skipped'. `sample_id` overrides the auto-detect.
tools.when_to_use: Return every CollectionScan for the currently-active sample. The active sample is the lowest-queue-order entry in plan_json's sample_queue whose status is not 'done' (or the explicit `active_sample_id` plan flag, if set). Pass `sample_id` to override the auto-detect.

tools.name: get_scans_since_last_plan_update
tools.description: Return the list of CollectionScan rows whose timestamp is newer than the live ExperimentPlan.updated_at. Lets the Planner see exactly what data has been collected since the plan was last revised.
tools.when_to_use: Return every CollectionScan row whose timestamp is newer than the live ExperimentPlan.updated_at. Used by the Planner to see what data has been collected since it last revised the plan. Sample names are joined in from SamplePosition. Read-only.

tools.name: get_staff_guidance
tools.description: Recent staff/user guidance messages — either typed into the dashboard guidance panel or posted to Slack.
tools.when_to_use: Recent staff / user guidance messages (Slack or web).
tools.sample_output: [{"timestamp": "2026-05-04T10:30:00Z", "author": "staff_jane", "text": "Use 1s count time for Fe samples, they are radiation sensitive."}]

tools.name: list_open_interventions
tools.description: List pause-for-human requests that are still waiting for staff to resolve.
tools.when_to_use: List pause-for-human requests still waiting.
tools.sample_output: [{"id": "intv_001", "kind": "sample_mount", "detail": "Mount holder B with Cu reference samples", "created_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z"}]

tools.name: recent_actions
tools.description: Most recent action_log entries for the current experiment — every SPEC-mutating tool call appears here. Also used by the phase-transition gate to verify prior success.
tools.when_to_use: Most recent action_log entries for the current experiment.
tools.sample_output: [{"id": 42, "timestamp": "2026-05-04T10:30:00Z", "phase": "collection", "command": "Fe_xas 1.0 5", "justification": "Collecting Fe K-edge XAS on sample Fe2O3_01", "success": true}]

tools.name: record_completed_scan
tools.description: Insert a CollectionScan row keyed by sample_id + scan_number after each successful run_xas (or sibling technique). Called by the Data Collection / Sample Surveyor agents between scans so the Planner's convergence analysis and the orchestrator's plan summary (recent_plots lookup) can see what's been collected. Auto-fills sample_id / scan_number / spec_datafile from the active context when omitted.
tools.when_to_use: Insert a CollectionScan row keyed by sample_id + scan_number after a successful run_xas (or sibling technique). Auto-fills `sample_id` from the active sample in plan_json, `scan_number` from get_scan_number, and `spec_datafile` from get_current_datafile when omitted. The scan row is what makes the run visible to the Planner's convergence analysis and to the orchestrator's plan summary (recent_plots lookup). Justification is required so the action is auditable.

tools.name: record_convergence_stats
tools.description: Store per-sample convergence statistics (cumulative CV, feature SEM, verdicts, feature window) so the orchestrator can render a live statistics trend plot on the dashboard.
tools.when_to_use: Store per-sample convergence statistics from the latest analysis run. The orchestrator reads these to auto-generate a statistics trend plot on the dashboard.

tools.name: record_sample_progress
tools.description: Update per-sample status (queued/in_progress/done/skipped/failed), SNR estimate, efficiency verdict, and reps completed. Preserves the rest of the plan.
tools.when_to_use: Update per-sample status (snr_estimate, efficiency_verdict, reps_completed, note).

tools.name: regenerate_plan
tools.description: Rebuild the sample plan from the DB while preserving per-sample progress (status, reps_completed, notes) and user overrides (thresholds, holder_budgets, total budget). Call this after a sample holder is added or edited.
tools.when_to_use: Rebuild the sample plan from the database while preserving per-sample progress (status, reps_completed, notes) and user overrides (thresholds, holder_budgets, budget). Call this after a new sample holder is configured or an existing one is edited.

tools.name: request_human_intervention
tools.description: Pause the agent and ask a human to perform a physical action (crystal install, sample mount, foil insert, hardware reset, or custom). Posts to Slack and blocks until staff resolves it from the dashboard or Slack.
tools.when_to_use: Pause the agent and ask a human to complete a physical action (crystal install, sample mount, foil insert, etc.). Posts to Slack and blocks until resolved.

tools.name: set_experiment_end_time
tools.description: Set Experiment.end_time. The planner's remaining-beamtime math is end_time - now(). Accepts ISO-8601 end_time or hours_from_now.
tools.when_to_use: Set the absolute end-of-beamtime timestamp on the active experiment. Accepts ISO-8601 `end_time` OR `hours_from_now` (one or the other, not both).

tools.name: set_holder_time_budget
tools.description: Set a default per-sample time budget for a whole sample holder. New samples inherit the default; when apply_to_existing is true (default), existing samples on that holder also pick up the change.
tools.when_to_use: Set a default per-sample time budget for an entire sample holder. Stored under the plan's holder_budgets so new samples inherit it; when apply_to_existing=true (default), existing samples on that holder also get the new count_time/reps. Can also set a stop_time (absolute deadline) or hours_remaining (relative deadline) on the holder row — the holder's collection should finish by that time.

tools.name: set_sample_time_budget
tools.description: Adjust the time budget for a single sample — change the per-rep count_time and/or the number of reps. Mode restricts the change to one of xas or emiss.
tools.when_to_use: Adjust the time budget for a single sample. Tweak any of: per-rep count_time_s, total reps, reps_per_spot (int = even split, list[int] = explicit per-spot), n_spots. Optionally restrict to one mode ('xas' or 'emiss').

tools.name: update_plan
tools.description: Replace the live experiment plan JSON wholesale. The structure is up to the agent, but downstream views expect a sample_queue + holder_budgets + budget shape.
tools.when_to_use: Replace the live experiment plan JSON (structure decided by the agent).

tools.name: upload_sample_alignment_results
tools.description: Persist Sample-Alignment agent outputs to SamplePosition rows. Stores per-sample stage boundaries (sx/sy/sz lo/hi), measured emission energy, suggested starting filter count, and count rate. Called once per sample after the alignment recipe completes.
tools.when_to_use: Persist Sample-Alignment agent results to SamplePosition. Stores per-sample stage boundaries (sx/sy/sz lo/hi), measured emission energy, suggested starting filter, and count rate. Called once per sample after the alignment recipe completes. Justification is required (write op).

tools.name: upload_sample_survey_results
tools.description: Persist Sample-Surveyor outputs to SamplePosition rows. filter_count overwrites xas_filter (so Data Collection picks it up); counts_per_sec is recorded as the survey reference rate. survey_energy_ev / notes are stored as-is. survey_completed_at is set to now().
tools.when_to_use: Persist Sample-Surveyor results to SamplePosition. For each entry, overwrites xas_filter with the filter_count and stores counts_per_sec, survey energy, and notes. Justification is required (this is a write).

tools.name: get_anchor
tools.description: Read the current tracking-anchor state from the SPEC session: energy + m1vert + Tz at anchor time, the constituent m1vert1/m1vert2/Tz1/Tz2 motor positions, the crystal id, and the SPEAR steering offset stored at anchor time. Also reports whether SPEAR has visibly drifted (monvtra moved since anchoring) and whether the crystal set has changed (which makes the anchor invalid for the current geometry). Read-only — no justification required.
tools.when_to_use: Read the current tracking anchor from the SPEC session: stored energy, m1vert/Tz (and their 1/2 constituents), crystal id, and SPEAR steering offset captured at anchor time. Also reports whether SPEAR has visibly drifted since the anchor was set, or whether the crystal set has changed (which would invalidate the anchor for the current geometry).

tools.name: get_beam_size
tools.description: Return the last-measured horizontal and vertical beam FWHM (mm) and the current beam-size mode (big/small/unknown) for each axis. Read-only — reads SPEC globals set by measure_beam_size / beamx / beamz.
tools.when_to_use: Return the last-measured horizontal and vertical beam FWHM (mm) and the current beam-size mode (big/small/unknown) for each axis.

tools.name: get_beam_status
tools.description: Compact snapshot of whether the beam is usable: SPEAR ring current, BL15 shutter state, gap ownership, and a beam_good boolean.
tools.when_to_use: SPEAR current + BL15 state + gap ownership + beam_good flag.

tools.name: get_counter
tools.description: Count for the specified time (default 0.5 s) and return one specific counter's value. Runs ct and extracts the named counter.
tools.when_to_use: Count for <count_time> seconds and return one specific counter's value.

tools.name: get_counts
tools.description: Count for the specified time (default 0.5 s) and return all counter values as a {name: value} map.
tools.when_to_use: Count for <count_time> seconds and return all counter values (I0, I1, vortDT, etc.).

tools.name: get_current_datafile
tools.description: Returns the active SPEC data file path via the DATAFILE global.
tools.when_to_use: Returns the active SPEC data file path (DATAFILE global).

tools.name: get_element
tools.description: Return the value of the SPEC variable ELEMENT — the currently active element for the experiment.
tools.when_to_use: Return the currently active element and all configured elements with their incident and emission energies.

tools.name: get_plotselected_counter
tools.description: Return the currently plot-selected counter mnemonic — the counter peak/cen will operate on after a scan, set by the most recent plotselect call. Resolves SPEC's DET global via cnt_mne(DET). Use after select_element or plotselect to confirm SPEC matches the counter the experiment config expects for this element.
tools.when_to_use: Return the currently plot-selected counter mnemonic — the counter peak/cen will operate on after a scan, set by the most recent plotselect. Resolves SPEC's DET global via cnt_mne(DET). Use after select_element or plotselect to confirm SPEC matches the expected counter.

tools.name: get_scan_number
tools.description: Return SPEC's current SCAN_N counter. Use get_current_datafile separately if you also need the active file name.
tools.when_to_use: Current SPEC_N and datafile.

tools.name: read_all_positions
tools.description: Read every motor's current position. Wraps SPEC's wa and parses the output into a {name → value} map.
tools.when_to_use: Read all motor positions (wa) with parsed name→value map.

tools.name: read_motor_position
tools.description: Read a single motor's position as a parsed float. Read-only — does not require a justification.
tools.when_to_use: Read a single motor's current position (parsed float).
tools.sample_output:
{"ok": true, "kind": "read", "result": {"value": 12199.998, "raw": "
            energy
            energy
User
 High    25000.000
 Current 12199.998
 Low     5100.000
Dial
 ..."}}

tools.name: abort_current_scan
tools.description: Send Ctrl-C to SPEC to abort whatever is running. Only call this after confirming a real problem — aborts are expensive and may leave hardware in a half-state.
tools.when_to_use: Send Ctrl-C to SPEC. Only after confirming a problem.

tools.name: align_beamline
tools.description: Run the full beamline alignment macro. Multi-minute: optimizes M1/M2, peaks mono pitch, aligns the mono slits, optimizes the B stage, zeros the pinhole, measures beam size. One-shot — refuses a re-run if the action_log shows it already succeeded this experiment.
tools.when_to_use: Run the full `align_the_beamline` macro. Multi-minute, optimizes M1/M2, peaks mono pitch, aligns mono slits, optimizes B stage, zeros pinhole, measures beam size. Only in phase beamline_alignment.

tools.name: align_xes_spectrometer
tools.description: Align the 7-crystal HERFD analyzer via run_spec_align. One-shot per experiment. Crystals arg selects a subset (e.g. '1234' aligns only crystals 1–4).
tools.when_to_use: Run `run_spec_align` to align the 7-crystal HERFD analyzer. Only in phase xes_alignment.

tools.name: big_beam
tools.description: Set the KB-mirror benders to the big-beam (mm-scale, standard) preset. Moves m1ubend/m1dbend/m2ubend/m2dbend to the configured big-beam values and tags both beamsize_mode axes as 'big'.
tools.when_to_use: Set the KB-mirror benders to the big-beam preset (mm-scale, standard configuration). Moves m1ubend/m1dbend/m2ubend/m2dbend to the configured big-beam positions and tags both beamsize_mode axes as 'big'.

tools.name: calibrate_mono
tools.description: Standard mono calibration: dscan energy ±15 eV over a reference foil, find the inflection, then call calibrate_mono + reset_gap. The tabulated edge energy must be within 5 eV of the current energy.
tools.when_to_use: Standard calibration: dscan energy ±15 eV around a reference foil, find the inflection, and call calibrate_mono + reset_gap. `tabulated_edge_ev` must be within 5 eV of current energy.
tools.comments: Wraps SPEC calibrate_mono (theta.mac:78); takes a single tabulated edge energy in eV (e.g. 11918.7 for Au K). Macro asserts 4500 < edge < 25000 and writes set mono $1 + set_theta A[crystal]. Operator must umv energy <measured_inflection> first.

tools.name: fit_emission_peak
tools.description: Fit the most recent (or specified) emission scan with the lab's Pseudo-Voigt+skew model and return the suggested emission energy in eV. Wraps the SPEC `get_HERFD_energy` macro, which shells out to /usr/local/projects/HERFD_energy/get_HERFD_energy.py and prints `Suggested new emission value is <eV>`. Does NOT move the spectrometer — the agent decides whether/how to apply the value (e.g. via a follow-up emiss move).
tools.when_to_use: Fit the most recent (or specified) emission scan with the lab's Pseudo-Voigt+skew model and return the suggested emission energy in eV. Does NOT move the spectrometer — the agent decides whether/how to apply the value. Wraps the SPEC `get_HERFD_energy` macro.

tools.name: measure_beam_size
tools.description: Knife-edge scan to measure horizontal and vertical beam FWHM. Multi-minute. Removes filters, ensures DATAFILE=alignment, runs centered knife-edge scans on Sx and Sz, stores results in the global beamsize[] array. Each axis supports 'big' (large mm-scale beam) or 'small' (~50um focused) modes — wrong mode produces artifacts.
tools.when_to_use: Knife-edge scan to measure horizontal and vertical beam FWHM. Multi-minute. Removes filters and ensures DATAFILE=alignment. Each axis can be measured in 'big' (false, ~mm-scale beam) or 'small' (true, ~50um focused) mode; wrong mode produces artifacts. Standard configuration is small_x=false, small_z=false.

tools.name: move_motor
tools.description: Absolute motor move (SPEC's umv). Motor must be on the current phase's allow-list — e.g. during sample_alignment only Sx/Sy/Sz are movable.
tools.when_to_use: Absolute motor move (umv). Motor must be on the current phase's allowlist.

tools.name: move_motor_relative
tools.description: Relative motor move (SPEC's umvr) — shift a motor by a delta from its current position. Same phase allow-list as move_motor.
tools.when_to_use: Relative motor move (umvr).

tools.name: mv_energy
tools.description: Move incident energy. Does NOT enable tracking — if you want the ID gap to follow the mono, call `tracking 1` (not currently exposed as a tool) before invoking this, or use run_align_shortcut / a dedicated macro.
tools.when_to_use: Move incident energy (tracking on; moves mono + gap).

tools.name: mv_knife_clear
tools.description: Move the sample stage so the knife-edge blades are clear of the beam. Fast move, but the diagnostic body may still partially clip the beam to I1 — prefer mv_knife_out before trusting I1 for upstream-optic alignment.
tools.when_to_use: Move the sample stage so the knife-edge blades are clear of the beam. Fast move, but the diagnostic body may still partially clip the beam to I1. Use mv_knife_out instead before trusting I1 for upstream-optic alignment.

tools.name: mv_knife_out
tools.description: Move the sample stage so the entire diagnostic tool is fully out of the beam path. Slower than mv_knife_clear (large Sr rotation) but unambiguous: nothing diagnostic-related is in the beam.
tools.when_to_use: Move the sample stage so the entire diagnostic tool is fully out of the beam. Slower than mv_knife_clear (large Sr rotation), but unambiguous: nothing diagnostic-related is in the beam path. Use this before optimizing upstream optics with I1.

tools.name: mv_pinhole
tools.description: Move the sample stage so the diagnostic-tool pinhole is in the beam. Sx/Sy/Sz/Sr are driven to the pinhole pose, plus any active pinhole_offset.
tools.when_to_use: Move the sample stage so the diagnostic-tool pinhole is in the beam. Used to set the sample reference position. Sx/Sy/Sz/Sr are driven to the pinhole pose (plus any active pinhole_offset).

tools.name: mv_plastic
tools.description: Move the sample stage so the diagnostic-tool plastic scatterer is in the beam. Used to generate elastic scatter for XES spectrometer alignment.
tools.when_to_use: Move the sample stage so the diagnostic-tool plastic scatterer is in the beam. Used to generate elastic scatter for XES spectrometer alignment.

tools.name: open_data_file
tools.description: Start a new SPEC data file (newfile). Used per-sample so each sample's data lives in its own .dat.
tools.when_to_use: newfile — start a new SPEC data file (per-sample).

tools.name: peak_mono_pitch
tools.description: LVDT-driven piezo optimization of the 2nd mono crystal pitch. Used as a fallback or pre-scan tune-up.
tools.when_to_use: LVDT-driven piezo optimization of the 2nd mono crystal pitch.

tools.name: plotselect
tools.description: Select which counter SPEC uses for plotting during scans. Use I1 for alignment optimization (downstream signal), vortDT for fluorescence, I0 for upstream flux monitoring. Does not affect data collection — only the live plot display.
tools.when_to_use: Select which counter SPEC plots during subsequent scans. Use I1 for alignment optimization, vortDT for fluorescence.

tools.name: post_scan_move
tools.description: After a scan, move the motor to the detected feature: 'cen' (center) or 'peak'. Run after a dscan/ascan, once the scan has been plotted and inspected (per agent-instructions §5), to land on the best point.
tools.when_to_use: Post-scan move: 'cen' (feature center) or 'peak' (feature peak).

tools.name: request_gap_ownership
tools.description: Blocking gaprequest — returns when SPEAR grants BL15 ownership of the ID gap, or when it times out.
tools.when_to_use: Blocking `gaprequest` — returns when SPEAR grants ownership or times out.

tools.name: reset_gap
tools.description: Recalibrate the undulator gap encoder zero. Runs ggg (gap dscan), peaks on the flux maximum, then redefines the gap encoder so the original (pre-scan) reading is preserved on the new peak. Run ONCE at the end of an energy-calibration sequence -- iterating reset_gap during calibration fights the calibrate_mono loop.
tools.when_to_use: Recalibrate the undulator gap encoder. Runs ggg (gap dscan), peaks on the flux maximum, then redefines the gap encoder so the original (pre-scan) reading is preserved on the new peak. Run ONCE at the end of an energy-calibration sequence -- iterating reset_gap during calibration fights the calibrate_mono loop.

tools.name: run_align_shortcut
tools.description: Run one of the named diagnostic shortcuts (vvv, hhh, m1m1, m2m2, ggg, bzbz, bxbx, dmm, beamx, beamz, cm1m1, cm2m2, beamx_fine, beamz_fine). Each is a single dscan + post-analysis.
tools.when_to_use: Run one of the named diagnostic shortcuts (vvv/hhh/m1m1/m2m2/ggg/bzbz/bxbx/dmm/beamx/beamz/cm1m1/cm2m2). Each is a single dscan+analysis.

tools.name: run_collection
tools.description: Multi-sample data-collection loop. Cycles through every enabled sample, producing one SPEC file per sample.
tools.when_to_use: Run `run_collection` — the multi-sample data collection loop that cycles through every enabled sample. Only in phase collection.

tools.name: run_diagonal_scan
tools.description: Two-motor diagonal scan (SPEC's d2scan) — both motors move in lockstep over the same delta range and number of points. Used during sample alignment to map a sample's footprint in the Sx/Sy plane (the d2scan in auto_sample_align's per-sample boundary detection). Default range is ±8 if delta_lo / delta_hi aren't provided. The dispatcher checks motor1 against the phase allow-list; the wrapper additionally validates motor2 before dispatch. NOTE: the `cen` scan-followup command does not work properly on a d2scan (2D scan) — do not rely on post_scan_move with mode='cen' after this scan; compute the center yourself and move explicitly instead.
tools.when_to_use: d2scan — relative scan of two motors moving in lockstep, each spanning the same delta range over the same number of points. Common use: map a sample's footprint in the Sx/Sy plane to find its edges (the staple of auto_sample_align's per-sample boundary detection). Default range is ±8. NOTE: the `cen` scan-followup command does not work properly on a d2scan (2D scan) — do not rely on `post_scan_move` with mode='cen' after this scan; compute the center yourself and move explicitly instead.

tools.name: run_emiss_scan
tools.description: Element-specific emission-energy scan (<element>_cee). Requires an emission_ev; filter is an optional 0-255 bitmask.
tools.when_to_use: Element-specific emission-energy (_cee) scan.

tools.name: run_motor_scan
tools.description: Absolute motor scan (ascan). Commonly used for alignment diagnostics where the motor absolute range matters.
tools.when_to_use: ascan — absolute motor scan.
tools.comments: doesnt allow .5 entry for count time, hangs when it should have been able to finish ok.

tools.name: run_motor_scan_relative
tools.description: Delta scan (dscan) centered on the motor's current position. Preferred for per-sample fine-tuning.
tools.when_to_use: dscan — delta scan around the current position.

tools.name: run_sample_alignment
tools.description: Run auto_sample_align: Sz survey plus per-sample centering. Populates each sample's Sx/Sy/Sz in the plan.
tools.when_to_use: Run `auto_sample_align`. Only in phase sample_alignment.

tools.name: run_xas
tools.description: Calls the SPEC run_xas macro, which dispatches to <El>_xas for the element set by the prior select_element. Args: cntSec (null→1 s), nbrScan (null→1), emission (0 → emiss motor unchanged), nbrFilter (<0 → filter motor unchanged).
tools.when_to_use: This command will call the _xas macro function for spectrum collection based on the element set by select_element. All args get passed onto the <El>_xas func: "<El>_xas  cntSec  nbrScan  emission  nbrFilter". Null value for cntSec defaults to 1s, nbrScan to 1, if emission is zero the emiss is not moved, if nbrFilter <0 then filter motor isnt moved. Optional `element` arg first runs select_element(<element>) for the convenience of single-call element switching.

tools.name: safely_remove_filters
tools.description: Ramp filters out via the XRS-safe macro — avoids the sample-damage risk of pulling all attenuators at once.
tools.when_to_use: Remove filters using the XRS-safe macro.

tools.name: select_element
tools.description: Switch the beamline to the configured per-element geometry — sets energy, emission energy, Vortex ROI, and runs xes_setup.
tools.when_to_use: Switch the beamline to the experiment's configured geometry for a single element (energy, emiss, Vortex ROI, xes_setup).

tools.name: set_anchor
tools.description: Capture mono/m1vert/m1vert1/m1vert2/Tz/Tz1/Tz2 (plus monvtra for SPEAR steering) as the tracking-anchor reference, and persist to anchor.cfg + a timestamped backup. The anchor is the fixed beam-position pivot that energy-tracking uses to keep the focused beam on the sample as the mono Bragg angle changes. Call once the beam is aligned at a known reference energy.
tools.when_to_use: Capture the current positions of mono (energy), m1vert/m1vert1/m1vert2, and Tz/Tz1/Tz2 (plus monvtra for SPEAR steering) as the tracking-anchor reference. Subsequent energy moves with tracking enabled use this anchor as the fixed beam-position pivot. Also writes the anchor to /usr/local/lib/spec.d/anchor.cfg and a timestamped backup. Call this once the beam is aligned at a known reference energy.

tools.name: set_filter
tools.description: Set the filter motor to a 0-255 bitmask (each bit is one filter pad). Used to attenuate the beam before high-flux scans.
tools.when_to_use: Set the filter motor (0-255 bitmask).

tools.name: set_gain
tools.description: Set the SRS current amplifier gain on I0/I1/I2. Accepts a string setting (e.g. '50 nA/V').
tools.when_to_use: Set I0/I1/I2 SRS gain (string, e.g. '50 nA/V').

tools.name: set_m2_stripe
tools.description: Move M2 (m2vert) to the appropriate stripe for the supplied incident energy. Branches: <4500 eV -> Rh (with a warning), 4500-6200 eV -> Si (m2vert=9.69), >=6200 eV -> Rh (m2vert=-3.5). Use whenever the incident energy crosses a stripe boundary.
tools.when_to_use: Move M2 (m2vert) to the correct stripe for a given incident energy. Below 4500 eV the macro defaults to the Rh stripe with a warning; between 4500 and 6200 eV it selects the Si stripe (m2vert=9.69); at or above 6200 eV it selects the Rh stripe (m2vert=-3.5). Use after moving incident energy across a stripe boundary.

tools.name: set_vortex_roi
tools.description: Set the Vortex ROI. mode='auto': bounds ±200 eV around the emission line for channel (1=vortDT, 3=vortDT2 for a second emission peak). mode='explicit': set channel + lo_ev/hi_ev in eV directly.
tools.when_to_use: Set Vortex ROI. mode='auto': bounds ±200 eV around the emission line for channel (1=vortDT, 3=vortDT2). mode='explicit': set channel + lo_ev/hi_ev in eV directly.

tools.name: shutter
tools.description: Fast-shutter control. fsopen/fsclose toggle the shutter; fson/fsoff enable/disable automatic shuttering; optional delay_s for timed opens.
tools.when_to_use: Fast-shutter control.

tools.name: small_beam
tools.description: Set the KB-mirror benders to the small-beam (~50um focused) preset. Moves m1ubend/m1dbend/m2ubend/m2dbend to the configured small-beam values and tags both beamsize_mode axes as 'small'.
tools.when_to_use: Set the KB-mirror benders to the small-beam preset (~50um focused). Moves m1ubend/m1dbend/m2ubend/m2dbend to the configured small-beam positions and tags both beamsize_mode axes as 'small'. After running, alignment routines and measure_beam_size should be invoked in their small-beam mode.

tools.name: tracking
tools.description: Toggle energy-tracking on (1) or off (0). With tracking enabled, every energy move also drives m1vert and Tz so the focused beam stays at the anchor position as the mono Bragg angle changes. Requires set_anchor first -- without an anchor, tracking has no reference and the beam will drift. Disable before procedures that need m1vert/Tz free of automatic motion (e.g. KB-mirror alignment).
tools.when_to_use: Enable or disable energy tracking. When enabled, every energy move also drives m1vert and Tz so the focused beam stays at the anchor position as the mono Bragg angle changes. Requires set_anchor to have been called first -- without an anchor, tracking has no reference and the beam will drift. Disable before procedures that should leave m1vert/Tz untouched (e.g. independent KB-mirror alignment).

tools.name: xtal_align
tools.description: Recalibrate the crystal-motor encoder zero. Runs a dscan over the crystal motor, peaks on the diffraction feature, then redefines the current encoder reading to the original (pre-scan) value. The motor stays in place; its zero is now anchored on the peak. Used after a crystal swap or when the crystal feature has drifted.
tools.when_to_use: Recalibrate the crystal motor encoder zero. Runs a dscan over the crystal motor, peaks on the diffraction feature, then redefines the current encoder reading to the original (pre-scan) value -- so the motor effectively stays in place but its zero is now on the peak. Use after a crystal swap or when the crystal feature has drifted.

tools.name: zero_pinhole
tools.description: Center the beam on the diagnostic-tool pinhole, then zero (or apply the configured pinhole_offset to) Tz/Sz/Bz/Tx/Sx/Bx. Multi-minute. Refuses to run if the table is not in its usual position (Tz < 15.5 with no offset configured).
tools.when_to_use: Center the beam on the diagnostic-tool pinhole, then zero (or apply the configured pinhole_offset to) Tz/Sz/Bz/Tx/Sx/Bx. Multi-minute. Refuses to run if the table is not in its usual position (Tz < 15.5 with no offset configured).

tools.name: steering_ack
tools.description: Set active_agent_ack_at on a steering row; optionally link agent_run_id. Defaults to $BEAMTIMEHERO_AGENT_RUN_ID if not provided.
tools.when_to_use: Acknowledge a pending steering row so the orchestrator knows the agent has seen it.

tools.name: steering_complete
tools.description: Mark a steering row complete: write result text and set completed_at timestamp.
tools.when_to_use: Mark a steering row as done after fulfilling the staff guidance.

tools.name: steering_defer
tools.description: Defer a steering row by writing 'deferred — <reason>' as ack_comment without setting completed_at. Optionally specify a target agent type for orchestrator re-dispatch.
tools.when_to_use: Defer a steering row when it cannot be handled now and should be re-dispatched later.

tools.name: steering_pending
tools.description: List pending steering rows (completed_at IS NULL) as a JSON array. Optionally filter by experiment or show only unacknowledged rows.
tools.when_to_use: List pending staff-guidance rows to see what needs to be acknowledged or completed.

tools.name: steering_set_comment
tools.description: Write or update the ack_comment field on a steering row.
tools.when_to_use: Add a comment to a steering row to record agent notes or status updates.

tools.name: action-log
tools.description: Action-log schema and how each CLI invocation is recorded.
tools.when_to_use: When operator asks about action-log schema and how each cli invocation is recorded.

tools.name: getting-started
tools.description: Quick overview of the beamtimehero CLI surface.
tools.when_to_use: When operator asks about quick overview of the beamtimehero cli surface.

tools.name: profiles
tools.description: Agent profiles: curated per-agent CLI surfaces over the master catalog.
tools.when_to_use: When operator asks about agent profiles: curated per-agent cli surfaces over the master catalog.
